response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with nearly two decades of experience, but the fit for this specific role is weak. His C++ experience is limited and dated (a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project on Linux), falling far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. He has no Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain experience — RIDE Capital was a retail investment fintech, not capital markets. SQL exposure appears general (MySQL/MongoDB) rather than deep SQL Server/Oracle work.
strengths: Long, diverse engineering career with strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Systems-level and low-latency experience (DLang async I/O at Dunnhumby, some C/C++ at National Instruments)
Excellent communication skills evidenced by workshops, talks, and teaching
concerns: C++ experience is brief, dated (2014–2015), and not Windows-platform focused
No Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain background
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle depth; database work centers on MongoDB/MySQL

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain experience
Strong general engineering fundamentals and communication
